dictionary_sections.definition: SymbolCanon is a TZPID dictionary-fill candidate anchored by the canonical relation Definition 04: ∇2 Ψ - ∂∂tΨ2 = JTZP ; 04. ∇2 Ψ - ∂∂tΨ2 = JTZP ; Definition 01: ΩT = ∇·1T ; Ω> = ∇ · Ft. It is not promoted content; it fills the currently empty dictionary slots from the folder's existing equation state and linked context.
dictionary_sections.contextual_meaning: In context, SymbolCanon names the operation or constraint expressed by the recovered equation. Source context: tion dictionary. --- ## Part Four: The Helical Phase Space (ID 1125) ### DNA Geometry as a Quantum Confinement Structure DNA is a double helix with precise geometric parameters: - **Major groove width**: approximately 22 Å - **Minor groove width**: approximately 12 Å - **Helical rise per base pair**: 3.4 Å - **Helical pitch**: 10.4 base pairs per full turn, or approximately 35 Å per turn The helical phase space projection (ID 1125) models the DNA substrate as a quantum confinement volume: $$\mathcal{P}_\text{helix} \in [16\,\text{Å},\,24\,\text{Å}] \times [0,\,2\pi n] \times \left[0,\,3.4\,\text{Å} \cdot 10^9\right]$$ The three dimensions of this phase space are: 1. **Radial groove dimension** $[16, 24\,\text{Å}]$: The range of groove widths where quantum coherence can survive against thermal noise. Too narrow, and interactions become too strong; too wide, and the confinement geometry breaks down.

\]

source_evidence_payload.context_excerpt: tion dictionary. --- ## Part Four: The Helical Phase Space (ID 1125) ### DNA Geometry as a Quantum Confinement Structure DNA is a double helix with precise geometric parameters: - **Major groove width**: approximately 22 Å - **Minor groove width**: approximately 12 Å - **Helical rise per base pair**: 3.4 Å - **Helical pitch**: 10.4 base pairs per full turn, or approximately 35 Å per turn The helical phase space projection (ID 1125) models the DNA substrate as a quantum confinement volume: $$\mathcal{P}_\text{helix} \in [16\,\text{Å},\,24\,\text{Å}] \times [0,\,2\pi n] \times \left[0,\,3.4\,\text{Å} \cdot 10^9\right]$$ The three dimensions of this phase space are: 1. **Radial groove dimension** $[16, 24\,\text{Å}]$: The range of groove widths where quantum coherence can survive against thermal noise. Too narrow, and interactions become too strong; too wide, and the confinement geometry breaks down.
